message:
feat(stress): add hostcall reactor diagnostics and shard comparison to stress harness

Stress harness (ext_stress binary):
- Add CLI flags: --reactor-enabled, --reactor-shards, --reactor-lane-capacity,
  --reactor-drain-budget for controlling reactor mesh during stress runs
- Add --compare-shard-baseline / --compare-baseline-shards to run A/B
  shard configurations back-to-back and emit a comparison report
- Refactor run loop into run_profile helper that handles warmup + measurement
- Configure reactor via new configure_reactor helper before each run
- Collect reactor queue depth samples at RSS intervals and drain reactor
  per dispatch iteration to keep queue telemetry bounded
- Emit reactor diagnostics (queue depths, stall reasons, migration events)
  and optional comparison section in the JSON stress report

Stress tests (extensions_stress.rs):
- Define ReactorQueueSample, ReactorDiagnostics structs for structured
  reactor telemetry collection during test runs
- Add build_reactor_diagnostics() to aggregate lane fallback reasons,
  marshalling fallback reasons, and lane migration events from the
  runtime hostcall telemetry artifact
- Enable reactor with 4 shards / 512 lane capacity in all stress
  test extension loads via maybe_enable_reactor()
- Drain reactor queue (budget=128) each dispatch iteration
- Sample queue depths at RSS sampling intervals
- Include reactor diagnostics in StressResult and stress reports
- Add post-run assertions verifying reactor fields in triage report
- Track reactor_rejected_enqueues and reactor_migration_event_total
  in policy profile rotation slices

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
